Is the research confined to Dementia only? I ask because when my Mum was in a Hospice before she died of Pancreatic cancer in 2004, she'd been sleeping a lot & didn't appear to be 'with it'. Two or three days before she died, we'd all gone to see her, as it was the weekend. Mum suddenly opened her eyes & said, "I want you all to say goodbye, because I'm going now. Then you must leave". We all looked at each other & Dad said we should do as she asked, so we did, but all congregated in the corridor. We decided one of us would continue to sit with her until the end. From that afternoon Mum wasn't there. Nothing. Silence. A couple of days later one of the Nurses asked Dad if he'd told Mum it was ok to go & if not, maybe now was the time. He stood at the bottom of the bed & told Mum it was ok to go, that he'd be fine, as would we. Within half an hour Mum died, peacefully. I'm convinced she heard him.

People with advanced dementia have been known to suddenly recognize loved ones before death. Scientists are now investigating why.

When faced with "financial advisers" I always like to click their profile and look at their track record. So Jeff Prestridge wrote an article on TROJAN GLOBAL EQUITY FUND proclaiming "managers really do their homework for long term gains" In 1 year returns since that article, Trojan had a return of -9.64%, which is really quite amazing as I'm not sure how they managed a negative return in a global stock boom. Global large caps are up 17% 1Y as a comparison. I looked at their portfolio - 5% to LSE, Heineken and a Spanish software firm that designs travel websites each. Wild. Let's go further, 1 year is not a lot for long term gains, true. 27/11/21 09:51 article on CERNO PACIFIC FUND "boutique investment house specialising in unearthing winning companies" 5Y ... down -6.08% ANNUALISED. After inflation you'd be like -60% down. Props to Prestridge, he's retired, so perhaps not actively researching. But for his readers - better if he stays retired.

He’s going to make a balls of what was a beautiful estate house and grounds. No class.

Tucked away in a bowl-shaped valley, surrounded by rolling hills, just miles from the Jurassic Coast, the quaint west Dorset town of Beaminster is simply picture perfect. But these quiet streets, home to only around 3,000 people, who leave their doors unlocked and know each other by name, have, in recent years, become slightly less so. Windows throughout Beaminster now bear stickers and signs saying 'No!' to planned development works at Parnham House, a derelict 131-acre estate to the south of the village. Former rave promoter James Perkins, 57, who set up nineties party organiser Fantazia, bought the property for £2.5million in 2020, with the aim of turning it into a hotel and events venue. But to raise £15million to help pay for it, the couple want to obtain and sell planning permission for a gated riverside community of 82 luxury homes on the stately home's parkland. The project has alarmed residents, who fear it will wreck biodiversity, interrupt their views, create no affordable housing for locals - and maybe even fail at saving the historic house.
  • 34 comments
  • share Tycoon at war with neighbours over plans to build 82 houses

Royal observers were delighted when Princess Margaret's grandson Sam Chatto announced his engagement to long-time girlfriend Eleanor Ekserdjian this week. The ceramicist, 29, proposed to the painter and filmmaker, also 29, who is known as Ellie, with a porcelain ring he crafted himself - and soon took to Instagram to tell fans the happy news. But while Sam, as a member of the Royal Family, might have some of the highest-profile relatives in the nation, Eleanor's background is just as fascinating. EXCLUSIVECelebrity chef Parveen Ashraf speaks out publicly for first time after husband of 38 years shot in cold blood in front of son in Ghana

Celebrity chef Parveen Ashraf shocked fans this week when she made a surprise return to social media, after around ten months, to share some devastating news. In a tearful video posted to Instagram, the cook, 61, known as Parveen the Spice Queen, told her some 30,000 followers that her husband had passed away around six months ago. We can now reveal, in a horrifying twist, that her husband of 38 years, Qamar Ashraf, was in fact shot in cold blood in front of his son in Ghana. He was 68. His family have now told the Daily Mail of their heartbreak at their devastating loss - which they are speaking about publicly for the first time.
  • 7 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Celebrity chef Parveen Ashraf speaks out after husband shot

Tyreece Clarke-Jones (pictured), 28, who raps under the name Tallerz, escaped from jail on May 23 while serving an eight-year sentence for robbery involving an axe and hammer. In a video shared this week, the criminal from Birmingham gives it large to the camera while rapping: 'I'm with bae bae, on a date night, I put my shank (knife) in her bra.' Another brazen video shared with his 45,000 followers shows a car being driven around sunny hills with the caption, 'I'm by the sea overseas run it up'. Derbyshire Constabulary shared an appeal after the rapper fled HMP Sudbury two weeks ago, calling on the public to help them catch him.

Good for her, not a bad investment and it looks pretty attractive too.

Louise Houston initially purchased the disused toilet block at an auction in Cheltenham in 2011 after noting it had potential to become a place she could stay while visiting family in the area. After spending £5,000 on legal and planning fees and between £45,000 and £50,000 on building costs, she managed to transform the former public looks into a stylish holiday home in the Gloucestershire town by 2019.

What happened to The Cheetah Girls - from dating a Kardashian to shunning the spotlight - as cast are embroiled in a catfight ahead of reunion film

The Cheetah Girls are set to reunite with the release of a fourth film, two decades after they first appeared on screens. Adapted from Deborah Gregory's novels of the same name, The Cheetah Girls followed high schoolers Galleria (Raven-Symoné), Chanel (Adrienne Bailon-Houghton), Aquanetta (Kiely Williams) and Dorinda (Sabrina Bryan), who made up an aspiring girl group. Last week, it was announced that a new film, The Cheetah Girls: Next Gen, would see the ladies reprise their 'cheetah-licious' roles to take a new group of Cheetahs to Africa to volunteer at a wildlife sanctuary.
  • 26 comments
  • share What happened to The Cheetah Girls?
